children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On completion of the land exchange under section 1206(a)(2), there shall be established a special resources management unit in the State consisting of certain Federal land managed by the Forest Service, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Crystal Springs Watershed Special Resources Management Unit”, dated June 2006 (referred to in this section as the “map”), to be known as the “Crystal Springs Watershed Special Resources Management Unit” (referred to in this section as the “Management Unit”).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Exclusion of certain land
children.label: B Exclusion of certain land
children.text: The Management Unit does not include any National Forest System land otherwise covered by subparagraph (A) that is designated as wilderness by section 1202.1

children.label: I
children.text: entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws;

children.label: II
children.text: location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and

children.label: III
children.text: disposition under all laws pertaining to mineral and geothermal leasing or mineral materials.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Clause (i)(I) does not apply to the parcel of land generally depicted as “HES 151” on the map.

children.label: A
children.text: to ensure the protection of the quality and quantity of the Crystal Springs watershed as a clean drinking water source for the residents of Hood River County, Oregon; and

children.label: B
children.text: to allow visitors to enjoy the special scenic, natural, cultural, and wildlife values of the Crystal Springs watershed.

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Force of law
children.label: B Force of law
children.text: The map and legal description filed under subparagraph (A) shall have the same force and effect as if included in this subtitle, except that the Secretary may correct typographical errors in the map and legal description.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: C Public availability
children.text: The map and legal description filed under subparagraph (A) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the Forest Service.

children.label: I
children.text: in accordance with the laws (including regulations) and rules applicable to units of the National Forest System; and

children.label: II
children.text: consistent with the purposes described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: only allow uses of the Management Unit that are consistent with the purposes described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Fuel reduction in proximity to improvements and primary public roads
children.label: B Fuel reduction in proximity to improvements and primary public roads
I
children.text: National Forest System land; or

children.label: II
children.text: private land adjacent to National Forest System land;

children.label: ii
children.text: in any area located not more than 400 feet from the Cooper Spur Road, the Cloud Cap Road, or the Cooper Spur Ski Area Loop Road; and

children.label: iii
children.text: on any other National Forest System land in the Management Unit, with priority given to activities that restore previously harvested stands, including the removal of logging slash, smaller diameter material, and ladder fuels.

children.label: A
children.text: New road construction or renovation of existing non-System roads, except as necessary to protect public health and safety.

children.label: B
children.text: Projects undertaken for the purpose of harvesting commercial timber (other than activities relating to the harvest of merchantable products that are byproducts of activities conducted to further the purposes described in paragraph (2)).

children.label: C
children.text: Commercial livestock grazing.

children.label: D
children.text: The placement of new fuel storage tanks.

children.label: E
children.text: Except to the extent necessary to further the purposes described in paragraph (2), the application of any toxic chemicals (other than fire retardants), including pesticides, rodenticides, or herbicides.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary may provide for the closure or gating to the general public of any Forest Service road within the Management Unit.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Nothing in this section requires the Secretary to close the road commonly known as “Cloud Cap Road”, which shall be administered in accordance with otherwise applicable law.

children.label: i
children.text: the owners of the private property; and

children.label: ii
children.text: guests to the private property.

children.heading: Cooperation
children.label: B Cooperation
children.text: The Secretary is encouraged to work with private landowners who have agreed to cooperate with the Secretary to further the purposes of this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may acquire from willing landowners any land located within the area identified on the map as the “Crystal Springs Zone of Contribution”.

children.heading: Inclusion in Management Unit
children.label: B Inclusion in Management Unit
children.text: On the date of acquisition, any land acquired under subparagraph (A) shall be incorporated in, and be managed as part of, the Management Unit.
